additional_comments: The mobile app crashes at least once a week. Really frustrating when I'm trying to check my budget on the go.
I switch between FinTrack and Monarch Money. FinTrack has better budgeting but Monarch's mobile app is way smoother.
My wife and I both use FinTrack but coordinating on the mobile app is a pain. We need better shared access.
Switched from YNAB but honestly the budget setup here took me three hours to figure out.
The AI keeps categorizing my meal prep groceries as dining out. Minor but adds up over a month.
I use Empower primarily for investment tracking since FinTrack just doesn't cut it there. Would consolidate if they improved it.
Would love push notifications for upcoming bills. I've missed two payments because the app didn't remind me.
When bank sync fails I have no idea where I stand on my debt payoff goal. It's broken like twice a month.
App is decent but loads slowly on my commute. Would be a 5 if it were faster.
Honestly stopped using the mobile app months ago. Just log in on my laptop now.
I wish there was a simpler mode for people just trying to get out of debt. Too many features I don't need.
My credit union isn't supported so I have to manually log everything. A better mobile entry form would help a lot.
Need to be able to track individual stock performance and dividends. The current net worth tracker is too basic.
We have five kids so managing the household budget is intense. The family features need a lot of work.
Bank sync breaks every time my bank updates security. Kills my trust in the data.
As a freelancer my income varies wildly month to month. The budget setup doesn't handle variable income well at all.
Please fix bank sync. I'm trying to pay off $28k in student loans and I need accurate data every day.
Love the AI categorization overall. Just wish it learned from my corrections faster.
We're comparing FinTrack and Monarch Money for family budgeting. Monarch's mobile is better but FinTrack's categories are more detailed.
The loading times on 4G are really bad. I've given up checking the app mid-commute.
If you added proper brokerage integration I would cancel Empower immediately.
The envelope budgeting concept was easier in YNAB. FinTrack feels more like tracking than planning.
Bill alerts would be a game changer for us. We have subscriptions all over the place.
Pretty happy with FinTrack. Copilot has a nicer interface but I like FinTrack's reporting more.
I have three different income streams and setting up the budget to reflect that took forever.
When I can't trust the numbers I feel like I'm flying blind on my debt payoff plan.
Saving for a down payment on a condo. I need this app to work perfectly on mobile since I check it every morning.
I use Empower for everything investment-related. Only keep FinTrack for expense tracking.
I started with YNAB but it was too expensive. FinTrack is more affordable but harder to learn.
My bank connection breaks at least twice a month. It's my biggest frustration with the app.
The net worth tracker is useful but I need portfolio performance tracking that goes beyond simple account balances.
The AI categorization has gotten so much better. Occasionally misreads subscriptions but mostly solid.
Would really appreciate a bill calendar widget for the phone home screen.
I check FinTrack first thing every morning. When bank sync is broken it ruins my whole morning routine.
It took me multiple YouTube videos to figure out how to set up a proper debt payoff plan in FinTrack.
The monthly budget model doesn't work for freelancers. I need a rolling or quarterly budget option.
My spouse and I can't view each other's transactions easily. Shared household view is missing.
Love the concept. The execution on mobile just needs a lot of polish.
The product is great on desktop. Mobile feels like an afterthought.
When sync breaks I feel like I've lost control of my finances. It's more stressful than helpful at that point.
Overall pretty satisfied. Bill alerts is the one thing I keep wishing for.
I track seven brokerage accounts and FinTrack just can't keep up with Empower for that use case.
Tried Copilot for a month. Came back to FinTrack but Copilot's mobile experience is significantly better.
AI categorization is the main reason I chose FinTrack over competitors. Keep investing in it.
I recommended FinTrack to two friends but warned them about the bank sync reliability issues.
Monarch's family features are better but FinTrack is cheaper. We're sticking with FinTrack for now.
I mainly use mobile to check my net worth quickly. Don't really need much else on mobile.
My coworkers all use different budget apps. I keep defending FinTrack but the mobile problems make it hard.
Overall I'm happy with FinTrack. Mobile could be faster but it gets the job done.
I'm in month four of my debt payoff plan and bank sync issues have thrown off my numbers three times.
We pay 14 subscriptions every month. Missing bill alerts has already cost us late fees twice.
I don't use the mobile app at all. Just Empower for my investments and FinTrack on desktop for expenses.
The debt snowball/avalanche options are buried too deep in the menus. I barely found them.
The app is so slow on older phones. Not everyone has the latest iPhone.
Really need a way to set a budget that accounts for months where I earn almost nothing.
Love the budget categories but the mobile interface needs a serious redesign.
Please add stock and ETF tracking. That's the one feature that would make FinTrack my single financial app.
Trying to save 20% of my income every month. I use FinTrack to keep myself honest but the mobile bugs undermine my confidence in the data.
I invoice clients on irregular schedules so my income is lumpy. FinTrack's budgeting assumes a steady paycheck.
I work two jobs and track every dollar. When sync breaks it throws off my whole debt timeline calculations.
I've had three bank connections break in the last two months. Almost switched apps because of this.
A family account with separate spending views for each family member would be really helpful.
The AI insights feature is promising. I'd pay a few dollars more a month if it got smarter.
Paying off $42k in credit card debt. This app is helpful but only when the data is accurate.
We have three kids in activities and managing the cash flow is stressful. Better bill tracking would help.
Living on a tight budget in a high cost-of-living city. Every dollar counts and I need this app to work reliably.
I'd love a widget for my phone lock screen showing today's spending against my daily budget.
Freelancer budgeting is just fundamentally different. FinTrack treats me like a salaried employee.
Good for expense tracking and budgeting. Just not useful for my investment portfolio.
The app is just too slow. I've timed it at 8 seconds to load my dashboard on LTE.
The AI spending insights are useful. I discovered I was spending $300/month on random Amazon purchases I hadn't tracked.
Copilot's mobile design is cleaner but FinTrack's budgeting depth is better. I wish you'd combine the two.
Please simplify the debt payoff setup. It shouldn't take a finance degree to get started.
I do consulting on the side of my main job. Tracking two income streams is awkward in FinTrack.
Two of my credit card accounts refuse to sync. I've contacted support three times with no fix.
A first-time homebuyer trying to track every dollar. The slow mobile app is genuinely hurting my ability to stay on top of my budget.
I've lost trust in the bank sync after too many failures. I now manually verify every transaction which defeats the purpose.
My number one request: make bank sync work consistently. Everything else is secondary.
I want FinTrack to feel like a premium mobile app not a mobile website. Right now it feels like the latter.
Would love the ability to log income at irregular intervals and have the app project my monthly total.
The AI review feature is my favorite part of FinTrack. I look forward to my weekly spending summary.
My student loan servicer keeps dropping from sync. Support told me it's a known issue but there's no ETA.
The gap between FinTrack and a proper investment tracking tool is huge. I hope you bridge it soon.
I've been freelancing for two years and have never found a budget app that truly works for variable income.
The app has gotten better over the past year but mobile is still lagging behind the web version by a lot.
I'm on a 36-month debt payoff plan. I need a tool that tracks progress automatically without me having to rebuild it every month.
Monarch Money is starting to look more attractive every time a bill alert fails to fire on FinTrack.
I work in tech so I know these things take time but bank sync reliability has to be the top priority.
Every time I show someone FinTrack they get confused by the budget setup. It really needs a guided onboarding flow.
A dedicated freelancer mode would probably make me recommend FinTrack to my entire network. Huge opportunity.
My kids are getting older and starting to spend money independently. Need a way to track their spending too.
I tell everyone that FinTrack is worth it but only once they get past the setup. That first week is brutal.
Built this into my morning routine but bank sync failures mean I can't trust the data about a third of the time.
Overall FinTrack is my go-to budgeting app. A better mobile experience and I'd recommend it to everyone without hesitation.
